title: Nexus C2 panel hosted on BtHoster (since pulled)
summary: An instance of the emerging multi-tenant Nexus C2 framework ran on confirmed BtHoster infrastructure with 40+ communicating Windows implants during an April-May 2026 burst, then was pulled to a maintenance page after public disclosure. Notable for placing a documented C2-as-a-service framework directly on an actively-rotating bulletproof operator.

iocs.role: Nexus C2 panel ("NEXUS — Login", uvicorn backend)

detail: The Nexus C2 panel (nexusc2[.]works) ran on 192[.]253[.]248[.]13 (AS213790, Limited Network LTD — the confirmed corporate identity behind BtHoster / BTCloud) with a wiki subdomain and wildcard cert, indicating it is sold as a C2-as-a-service. VirusTotal showed the domain at 13/91 with 40+ communicating Windows implants (stager.exe / client.exe / process-masquerade names), first-seen 2026-04-19 to 2026-05-12 — a ~3-week burst that stopped after public disclosure (newtonpaul, May 2026), after which the node was pulled to a maintenance page. Public reporting tied the panel to "Limited Network LTD" as a hosting name; the additional finding here is that Limited Network LTD = BtHoster, an actively-rotating bulletproof operator co-hosting a pan-EU phishing hub. A title-only fingerprint for sibling nodes proved too generic (FastAPI/uvicorn + the common name "Nexus"), so no live sibling C2 was validated.

references.label: newtonpaul — Hunting (AI-generated) C2 Part 2: Nexus C2, Shipped with Creds
